title: This play from Drake Maye’s rookie season continues to stand out to new Patriots safety
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye impressed Patriots safety Mike Brown during a standout rookie play against the Tennessee Titans in 2024.
In the 2025 season, Maye led New England to a surprise Super Bowl appearance, earning MVP candidacy.
New Patriots tight end Julian Hill praises Maye's leadership and rapport-building during offseason workouts.

title: Worcester man goes viral for performance protesting potholes at city council meeting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Wilson Lam's theatrical protest about potholes at a Worcester City Council meeting went viral with over 402,200 TikTok views.
Lam's performance, inspired by an Australian town hall video, highlights pothole issues dating back over two decades in Worcester.
Beyond potholes, Lam plans to address homelessness and drug issues at future council meetings and possibly run for mayor in 2027.

title: Jared Wilson’s switch back to center is a ‘full-circle moment’ with Drake Maye
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jared Wilson will move to center for the New England Patriots in the 2026 season.
Wilson returns to his original position after playing left guard during his rookie season.
Wilson previously played center at the University of Georgia, earning Second-Team All-SEC honors.

title: Karen Read’s Lexus SUV sold in private deal after low turnout canceled auction
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 was scheduled for auction in Woburn but sold privately due to low turnout.
JJ Manning Auctioneers required attendees to have valid ID and $20,000 bank funds for the Lexus auction.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges but faces a wrongful death lawsuit linked to the SUV incident.

title: Bruins re-sign Lukas Reichel to a 1-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins re-signed forward Lukas Reichel to a one-year contract with a $950,000 NHL cap hit.
Reichel, acquired from Vancouver in March, scored three goals and eight points over 29 NHL games last season.
Reichel, representing Germany at the World Championships in Switzerland, must clear waivers if not on Bruins' roster.

title: Boston city councilor criticizes ‘soft on crime’ progressives after Cambridge shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Councilor Ed Flynn criticized progressives for being 'soft on crime' after a shooting in Cambridge.
Tyler Brown, 46, accused in the Cambridge incident, has a history of violent offenses and is on probation.
Flynn argued cases like Brown's erode trust in the justice system, calling for stricter sentencing for repeat offenders.

title: ‘Quality of life is plummeting’: Readers share their economic concerns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: 42% of 854 Massachusetts residents polled by MassINC report being worse off financially than one year ago.
Boston.com readers cite taxes, housing, and healthcare as major economic concerns affecting their quality of life.
Study shows Massachusetts households need income between $69,885 and $209,656 to be considered middle class.

title: A ‘lifestyle-focused’ restaurant opens Friday at this new Cape Cod hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Gypsy Sol restaurant opens May 15 at The Uncommoner Hotel in West Yarmouth, offering lifestyle-focused programming and diverse menu offerings.
The restaurant will feature Mediterranean-inspired small plates and cocktails, with dishes like za'atar seared tuna and Gypsy Sol Oysters.
Additional offerings include live music, yoga, tarot readings, and Arcana Cafe serving coffee and pastries within the restaurant.

title: 18-year-old arrested in connection to Mother’s Day double shooting in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jaynell Sumler, 18, charged with two counts of assault to murder, linked to Dorchester double shooting.
Shooting at 69 Kingsdale St. wounded a woman critically and injured a man non-critically.
Sumler also charged with firearm offenses; pleaded not guilty in court Thursday morning.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ was renewed for Season 2
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Bravo renewed 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' for a second season, announced on May 11.
RHORI is Bravo's second-biggest show across platforms, only behind 'The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills.'
No specific cast or air dates for RHORI Season 2 have been confirmed as of now.
